textPlain: Why does air-gapped environment require rolling your own CI/CD solution? Plenty of examples of air-gapped Jenkins and/or Argo Workflows. Was this just an educational exercise?

Jenkins sucks but is insanely reliableArgo Workflows does not live up to what they advertise, it is so much more complex to setup and then build workflows for. Helm + Argo is pain (both use the same template delimiters...)

It seems like a simple CI/CD in an airgapped environment might be simpler to implement than to (1) learn and (2) onboard an off-the-shelf solution when your airgapped requirement limits your ability to leverage the off-the-shelf ecosystem.
This was more like an educational exercise
